'''Jonathan Lomax''' (born 4 September 1990) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in the [[Super League]],<ref name="Profile at loverugbyleague.com"/><ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> and [[England national rugby league team|England]] and [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] at international level. A versatile player, Lomax is primarily a {{rlp|so}} but is equally comfortable at both {{rlp|sh}} and {{rlp|fb}}, and featured more regularly on the {{rlp|wg}} in the early stages of his professional career.

==Early life==
Lomax was born in [[Billinge, Merseyside|Billinge]], Lancashire, England on 4 September 1990.

He played as a junior for Orrell St James, where he captained the side for some years, playing in his preferred {{rlp|so}} position. He attended [[Rainford High Technology College]] in [[St Helens, Merseyside|St Helens]] during his high school years. Lomax was injured quite seriously during a Year 9 Powergen Cup (Schools National Cup) quarter-final match, whereby he and an opposing player were both knocked unconscious in a collision. After regaining consciousness, he began vomiting and was taken to hospital, where he had to undergo emergency open skull surgery in order to relieve the pressure on his brain. Since then, Lomax has been synonymous with a [[Scrum cap|protective headgear]] that he still wears in his professional career as a message to other youth players of the dangers of head injuries.<ref name="BBC Rugby League podcast - The Jonny Lomax Story">{{Cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/programmes/p0hwpdmz|title=BBC Rugby League podcast - The Jonny Lomax Story}}</ref>

After his accident, Lomax continued his amateur career with Orrell and signed for St Helens professionally as a sixteen-year-old in 2006, after being on their books as a scholarship player previously.{{cn|date=October 2022}}

==St Helens==
Lomax made his first-team début for St. Helens in 2009's [[Super League XIV]] game against [[Wakefield Trinity]] in 2009, where he helped them to an easy 42–18 win.<ref name="Saints 42-18 Wakefield">{{Cite web|url=http://saintsrlfc.com/results/94|title=Saints 42–18 Wakefield|publisher=saintsrlfc.com|date=1 July 2009|access-date=1 July 2009}}</ref> Following his début, he played and scored in the 66–6 [[Challenge Cup 2009|Challenge Cup]] victory over [[Newcastle Thunder|Gateshead]] again playing on the wing in a [[Man of the Match#Rugby league and rugby union|man-of-the-match]] performance.<ref name="Saints 66-6 Gateshead">{{Cite web|url=http://saintsrlfc.com/results/119|title=Saints 66–6 Gateshead|publisher=saintsrlfc.com|date=1 July 2009|access-date=1 July 2009}}</ref> He notched the first try of his Super League career in a 30–6 win over [[Hull F.C.]] whilst playing on the right wing in June.<ref name="Saints 30-6 Hull FC">{{Cite web|url=http://saintsrlfc.com/results/102|title=Saints 30–6 Hull FC|publisher=saintsrlfc.com|date=1 July 2009|access-date=1 July 2009}}</ref> He took his try tally to three in four games for the Saints against [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]], where he was again named man-of-the-match in a 50–10 win.<ref name="Saints 50-10 Castleford">{{Cite web|url=http://saintsrlfc.com/results/103|title=Saints 50–10 Castleford|publisher=saintsrlfc.com|date=1 July 2009|access-date=1 July 2009}}</ref>
After a spell in the reserves, he played in the 40–26 victory against [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] where he came off the bench to play in a [[Rugby league positions#dummy half|dummy-half]] role.<ref name="Saints 40-26 Warrington">{{Cite web|url=http://www.saintsrlfc.com/results/107|title=Saints 40–26 Warrington|publisher=saintsrlfc.com|date=11 July 2009|access-date=14 July 2009}}</ref> He featured, albeit in a brief spell, for a second time in the season against [[Wakefield Trinity]] in a shock 22–20 loss. He was injured during this match and was out of action for 6 weeks.<ref name="Saints 20-22 Wakefield">{{Cite web|url=http://www.saintsrlfc.com/results/108|title=Saints 20–22 Wakefield|publisher=saintsrlfc.com|date=19 July 2009|access-date=22 July 2009}}</ref> On his return, he played in a left {{rlp|CE}} role in a 12–10 win over [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]].<ref name="Saints 12-10 Huddersfield">{{Cite web|url=http://www.saintsrlfc.com/results/112|title=Saints 12–10 Huddersfield|publisher=saintsrlfc.com|date=21 August 2009|access-date=22 August 2009}}</ref> He once again featured at full-back in the 24–12 end-of-regular-season loss to [[Catalans Dragons]], where he kicked two goals from as many attempts.<ref name="Saints 12-24 Catalans">{{Cite web|url=http://www.saintsrlfc.com/results/112|title=Saints 12–24 Catalans|publisher=saintsrlfc.com|date=11 September 2009|access-date=17 September 2009}}</ref> Lomax did not feature for St Helens in the end-of-season play-offs, which culminated in a third [[2010 Super League Grand Final|Grand Final]] loss to [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]].

Lomax's opportunities in the first team continued in 2010, with his first game of the season coming in a 38–6 win at [[Bradford Bulls]] in [[2010 Super League season results#Round 2|round 2]] of 2010's [[Super League XV]], where he featured on the right wing in place of the injured [[Ade Gardner]].<ref name="Saints 38-6 Bradford">{{Cite web|url=http://saintsrlfc.com/results/132|title=Saints 38–6 Bradford|publisher=saintsrlfc.com|date=15 February 2010|access-date=15 February 2010}}</ref> It was this Gardner injury that meant Lomax notched his maiden brace of tries in first-grade football, when he scored twice in a 42–12 win at [[Catalans Dragons]].<ref name="Saints 42-12 Catalans">{{Cite web|url=http://saintsrlfc.com/news/2314|title=Saints 42–12 Catalans|publisher=saintsrlfc.com|date=20 February 2010|access-date=21 February 2010}}</ref> Lomax's form was looking threatening in this spell, and he took his tally to three in four games with a score in the 22–16 home victory over the  Wakefield Trinity Wildcats in the round 4 game.<ref name="Saints 22-16 Wakefield">{{Cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3217251|title=Saints 22–16 Wakefield|publisher=superleague.co.uk|date=26 February 2010|access-date=12 March 2010}}</ref> However, in the round 5 defeat by [[Hull Kingston Rovers]],<ref name="Saints 24-28 Hull KR">{{Cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3217259|title=Saints 24–28 Hull KR|publisher=superleague.co.uk|date=7 March 2010|access-date=12 March 2010}}</ref> not only did Lomax's run of tries end, but his in the first team ended with a shoulder injury that meant he missed 6 weeks of action, which was ironically subsided by Gardners' return from his particular injury.<ref name="Wellens out of Crusaders clash">{{Cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/article.php?id=16857|title=Wellens out of Crusaders clash|publisher=superleague.co.uk|date=10 March 2010|access-date=12 March 2010}}</ref> On return, he played wing in a 42–34 loss away to [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]],<ref name="Saints 34-42 Salford City Reds">{{Cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3217312|title=Saints 34–42 Salford City Reds|publisher=superleague.co.uk|date=15 May 2010|access-date=22 May 2010|archive-date=8 August 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170808153549/http://www.superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3217312|url-status=dead}}</ref> and fullback, in a 68–12 win over Rovers, scoring one try and kicking three goals.<ref name="Saints 68-12 Hull KR">{{Cite web|url=http://superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3217317|title=Saints 68–12 Hull KR|publisher=superleague.co.uk|date=21 May 2010|access-date=22 May 2010|archive-date=8 August 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170808153812/http://superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3217317|url-status=dead}}</ref> His first appearance in the [[2010 Challenge Cup]] came in the quarter-final 32–12 win over [[Barrow Raiders|Barrow]], where Lomax continued his try-scoring form and run as chief goal kicker with one try and three goals from five attempts.<ref name="Saints Into Final Four">{{Cite web|url=http://www.saintsrlfc.com/news/2563|title=Saints Into Final Four|publisher=saintsrlfc.com|date=29 May 2010|access-date=29 May 2010}}</ref> He shifted back to the wing for a 27–26 loss at Hull, where Lomax kicked three out of five again.<ref name="Saints 26-27 Hull FC">{{Cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3217326|title=Saints 26–27 Hull FC|publisher=superleague.co.uk|date=4 June 2010|access-date=5 June 2010}}</ref> He missed the next two matches due to injury, but kicked nine out of ten goals in a return at home to Salford, where he was on the winning side in a 58–34 success,<ref name="Wellens Grabs Four As Saints Win Again">{{Cite web|url=http://www.saintsrlfc.com/news/2641|title=Wellens Grabs Four As Saints Win Again|publisher=saintsrlfc.com|date=25 June 2010|access-date=26 June 2010}}</ref> and scored one try and kicked four goals in a 28–24 loss to [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]].<ref name="Saints 24-28 Leeds">{{Cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3235944|title=Saints 24–28 Leeds|publisher=superleague.co.uk|date=3 July 2010|access-date=5 July 2010}}</ref> He kicked two goals in a 20–30 loss to Catalans Dragons,<ref name="Saints 20-30 Catalans">{{Cite web|url=http://saintsrlfc.com/results/151|title=Saints 20–30 Catalans|publisher=saintsrlfc.com|date=9 July 2010|access-date=18 July 2010}}</ref> and four goals in a 32–18 win over Harlequins the week later.<ref name="Saints 32-18 Harlequins">{{Cite web|url=http://superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3250716|title=Saints 32–18 Harlequins|publisher=superleague.co.uk|date=18 July 2010|access-date=18 July 2010}}</ref> Lomax continued his run in the St Helens first team and played on the wing in their [[2010 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]].
[[File:Jonny Lomax England.jpg|thumb|right|Lomax warming up for England in 2016|200px]]
Lomax enjoyed a breakthrough 2011 season, forming a makeshift halfback partnership with fellow youngster [[Lee Gaskell]] that saw St Helens reach another [[2011 Super League Grand Final|Grand Final]], however they were unsuccessful, once again beaten by the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2011/oct/08/super-league-grand-final-match-report?newsfeed=true|title=Leeds claim Grand Final glory as inspired Rob Burrow sinks St Helens|date=8 October 2011|work=Guardian|access-date=21 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/8815944/St-Helens-16-Leeds-32-match-report.html|title=St Helens 16 Leeds 32|date=8 October 2011|work=Daily Telegraph|access-date=21 November 2019}}</ref>

Lomax was handed the No. 7 jersey in 2012 as a result and enjoyed another good season, scoring 14 tries as the club's first-choice scrum-half, however Saints' run of successive Grand Final appearances came to an end as they were beaten by Warrington in the qualifying semi-final.

In 2013, Lomax was moved to the fullback position by coach [[Nathan Brown (rugby league born 1973)|Nathan Brown]], and played 19 times, scoring 12 tries and kicking 25 goals as St Helens once again narrowly missed out on a Grand Final appearance. In 2014 Lomax played in the first 13 matches for St Helens before being ruled out for the remainder of the season after suffering a severe knee injury in a 42–0 defeat against [[Catalans Dragons]]. Prior to this, his form at fullback had seen him earn a call up to the [[England national rugby league team|England]] squad for their mid-season training camp.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2014-06-17 |title=St Helens full-back Jonny Lomax ruled out for season with knee injury |url=http://www.theguardian.com/sport/2014/jun/17/st-helens-jonny-lomax-ruled-out-season-knee-injury |access-date=2022-11-15 |website=the Guardian |language=en}}</ref> As a result of his injury, Lomax missed St Helens' [[2014 Super League Grand Final]] victory.

Lomax returned at the start of the 2015 season, but lasted only four games before injuring the same knee, once again ruling him out for the entire season.<ref>[Lomax facing lengthy injury lay-off http://www.sthelensreporter.co.uk/sport/saints/lomax-facing-lengthy-injury-lay-off-1-7158297]</ref> His continued absence, along with the retirement through injury of [[Lance Hohaia]] and [[Paul Wellens]], forced St Helens to bring in several short-term replacements, including [[Shannon McDonnell (rugby league)|Shannon McDonnell]] and [[Adam Quinlan]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2015-07-01 |title=Adam Quinlan joins St Helens from Parramatta amid full-back crisis |url=http://www.theguardian.com/sport/2015/jul/01/adam-quinlan-st-helens-parramatta-full-back-crisis |access-date=2022-11-15 |website=the Guardian |language=en}}</ref>

He played in the [[2019 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by the [[Warrington Wolves]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="St Helens 4-18 Warrington RESULT: Challenge Cup Final as it happened from Wembley">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/st-helens-vs-warrington-live-18996678|title=St Helens 4–18 Warrington RESULT: Challenge Cup Final as it happened from Wembley|work=Daily Mirror|date=24 August 2019|access-date=1 November 2019}}</ref>

He played in the [[2019 Super League Grand Final]] victory over the [[Salford Red Devils]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="St Helens give Justin Holbrook the perfect send-off with commanding Grand Final victory over Salford">{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/rugby-league/2019/10/12/st-helens-vs-salford-red-devils-super-league-grand-final-live/|title=St Helens give Justin Holbrook the perfect send-off with commanding Grand Final victory over Salford|work=The Daily Telegraph|date=12 October 2019|access-date=25 October 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2019/oct/12/st-helens-v-salford-red-devils-super-league-grand-final-live/|title=St Helens 23–6 Salford Red Devils: Super League Grand Final – as it happened|website=The Guardian|date=12 October 2019|last1=Unwin|first1=Will}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/11777123/st-helens-win-2019-super-league-league-leaders-shield/|title=St Helens win 2019 Super League League Leaders' shield|website=Sky Sports}}</ref>

Lomax played in the club's 8-4 [[2020 Super League Grand Final]] victory over Wigan at the [[Kingston Communications Stadium]] in Hull.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/55072997|title=Super League Grand Final: Wigan 4-8 St Helens|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>

On 17 July 2021, Lomax played for St. Helens in their 26-12 [[2021 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over Castleford.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/st-helens-celebrate-challenge-cup-21081897/|title=How St Helens players have celebrated their first Challenge Cup win in 13 years|website=www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk|access-date=18 July 2021|archive-date=18 July 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210718102540/https://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/st-helens-celebrate-challenge-cup-21081897|url-status=dead}}</ref>
On 9 October 2021, Lomax played for St. Helens in their [[2021 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[Catalans Dragons]].  Lomax set up the winning try in the second half of the final after he put a short kick through for teammate [[Kevin Naiqama]] to score.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/58858850|title=Super League Grand Final: Catalans Dragons 10-12 St Helens: Saints win three in a row|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>
In round 18 of the 2022 Super League season, Lomax scored the winning try for St Helens with only one minute remaining as the club defeated [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] 20-18 at [[Magic Weekend]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/live-blog/12196/12648675/super-league-magic-weekend-day-1-at-newcastles-st-james-park-live/|title=Saints, Toulouse and Leeds all win on Magic Weekend|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>
On 24 September 2022, Lomax played in St Helens 2022 Super League Grand Final victory over [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] and was awarded the [[Harry Sunderland Trophy]] as man of the match.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2022/sep/24/st-helens-leeds-super-league-grand-final-match-report-rugby-league/|title=St Helens sink Leeds to win record fourth consecutive Grand Final|newspaper=The Observer|date=24 September 2022 |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron }}</ref>
On 18 February 2023, Lomax played in St Helens 13-12 upset victory over [[Penrith Panthers|Penrith]] in the [[2023 World Club Challenge]].<ref>{{cite news |title=St Helens stun Penrith to win World Club Challenge |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/64666651 |work=BBC Sport}}</ref>
Lomax played 29 games for St Helens in the 2023 Super League season as the club finished third on the table. Lomax played in St Helens narrow loss against the Catalans Dragons in the semi-final which ended St Helens four-year dominance of the competition.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sthelensstar.co.uk/sport/23856044.paul-wellens-q-saints-review-year-look-2024/|title=Paul Wellens Q&A: Saints review of year and a look to 2024|website=www.sthelensstar.co.uk|date=14 October 2023 }}</ref>
In round 9 of the 2024 Super League season, Lomax kicked the winning drop goal for St Helens as they won 13-12 over Huddersfield with less than a minute remaining.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/video/33731/13123017/jonny-lomax-wins-it-for-st-helens-to-break-huddersfield-giants-hearts/|title=Jonny Lomax wins it for St Helens to break Huddersfield Giants' hearts|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>
Lomax played 22 matches for St Helens in the 2024 Super League season which saw the club finish sixth on the table. He played in St Helens golden point extra-time playoff loss against Warrington.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2024/sep/28/warrington-st-helens-super-league-eliminator-playoff-rugby-league-match-report/|title=
‘Soul destroying’: Warrington eliminate St Helens in golden drop goal thriller|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>
Lomax played 26 games for St Helens in the 2025 Super League season including the clubs semi-final loss against Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c04q6rqyrv6o/|title=Retiring Waerea-Hargreaves set to miss Grand Final|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
In round 2 of the 2026 Super League season, Lomax was taken off within the first five minutes during St Helens narrow win over Leigh. It was later revealed that Lomax had dislocated his wrist and would be out for an extended period.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.totalrl.com/injury-corner-st-helens-quadruple-blow-hull-kr-pair-out-as-bradford-and-castleford-lose-key-men/|title=Injury Corner: St Helens, Hull KR, Hull FC and Castleford blows|website=www.totalrl.com}}</ref>

==International career==
Lomax was selected in England's 24-man squad for the [[2016 Rugby League Four Nations|2016 Four Nations]]. He made his international début in a [[2016 Rugby League Four Nations#France vs England|test match]] against France.

In October 2017 he was selected in the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup squads#England|England squad]] for the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="England rugby league World Cup squad revealed - and James Roby is back in the fold">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/james-roby-back-fold-wayne-11312505|title=England rugby league World Cup squad revealed – and James Roby is back in the fold|work=Daily Mirror|date=9 October 2017|access-date=30 October 2019}}</ref>

He was selected in squad for the [[2019 Great Britain Lions tour]] of the Southern Hemisphere.<ref name="Zak Hardaker shock inclusion in 24-man Great Britain squad for tour of New Zealand and Papua New Guinea">{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/rugby-league/2019/10/14/zak-hardaker-shock-inclusion-24-man-great-britain-squad-tour/|title=Zak Hardaker shock inclusion in 24-man Great Britain squad for tour of New Zealand and Papua New Guinea|work=The Daily Telegraph|date=14 October 2019|access-date=29 October 2019}}</ref> He made his [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] test debut in the defeat by Tonga.<ref name="Great Britain left bruised at Tonga party">{{cite web|url=https://www.thetimes.com/article/great-britain-tonga-rugby-league-hzmmhr5q5|title=Great Britain left bruised at Tonga party|work=[[The Times]]|date=26 October 2019|access-date=29 October 2019}}</ref>

==Personal life==
In 2024 he graduated with a first-class degree in Physiotherapy from the [[University of Salford]].<ref>{{cite news |title=Jonny Lomax graduates with first class honours from Salford |url=https://www.sthelensstar.co.uk/sport/24446205.jonny-lomax-graduates-first-class-honours-salford/ |access-date=28 October 2025 |work=St Helens Star |date=11 July 2024}}</ref>
